entries.title: Animal Realm and Primate Spirit Garden
entries.stamp: broken haniwa face inside a square seal
entries.thesis: Military geometry tries to contain souls that refuse to become identical.
entries.silhouette: stacked black towers, assembly lines, regimented haniwa ranks, enormous beast-shadow cutouts
layers: white void with sparse descending soul commas
factory terraces and distant regiment silhouettes
hard-edged platforms, clay ramps and conveyor seams
passing haniwa shields and beast claws
entries.landmark: Keiki atelier: a white goddess-shaped gap surrounded by dense black scaffolding
entries.texture: orthogonal 50% ordered dither interrupted by organic soul curves
entries.ambience: kiln pulse, marching dust, soul commas and one-frame forge sparks
entries.transition: a haniwa stamp strikes; the screen breaks into four disciplined panels
world_states: CALM: orderly ranks with one figure subtly misaligned
INCIDENT: command banners double and corridors narrow
ROUTE: one handmade figure appears beside the chosen character
SEASON: kiln steam and clay moisture change the floor pattern
AFTER: regiment gaps become gardens without losing the industrial skeleton
entries.combat_readability: reserve a clean 208x128 arena; hazards move on visible 16-pixel lanes; soul bullets never share the clay dither frequency
entries.art_anchor: angular city blocks, beast emblems, oil surfaces, clay figures, fortress garden walls ### Dither allocation - 0%: important white space, sky, or quiet emotional focus - 25%: distant atmosphere - 50%: traversable but uncertain space - 75%: danger, rumor, or occluded depth - 100%: solid silhouette and collision-critical geometry Never use high-frequency dither directly behind bullets or dialogue text.

entries.title: Eientei and the Bamboo Forest of the Lost
entries.stamp: rabbit ears crossing a medicine vial
entries.thesis: Repeated corridors become intimate only through tiny, remembered differences.
entries.silhouette: vertical bamboo bars, long rabbit ears, low tiled roofs and an impossible moon disc
layers: moon and sparse bamboo ghosts
two scrolling bamboo bands at unequal speed
root paths, clinic floor and sliding-door thresholds
near bamboo wipes, drifting leaves and rabbit-ear peeks

entries.texture: low-frequency vertical hatch; interiors return to broad white tatami fields
entries.ambience: bamboo sway, ear silhouettes, vial glints and moon phase wipes
entries.transition: three bamboo trunks pass foreground; the middle trunk becomes a door frame
world_states: CALM: readable leaf notches identify loops
INCIDENT: notches migrate and the moon repeats
ROUTE: a private shortcut gains a character-specific charm
SEASON: snow, rain and fireflies alter only the far/mid bands
AFTER: wrong turns retain small gifts instead of punishment
